message:
docs(migration): add fs/process/signal migration playbook with executable recipes (asupersync-2oh2u.3.8)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,228 @@
+{
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-2oh2u.3.8",
+  "title": "[T3.8] Migration playbook for fs/process/signal users",
+  "version": 1,
+  "generated_at": "2026-03-04",

+    "asupersync-2oh2u.3.3",

+    "asupersync-2oh2u.3.10"
+  ],
+  "domains": ["filesystem", "process", "signal", "cross-domain"],
+  "migration_recipes": [
+    {

+      "id": "MR-FS-02",

+      "change_type": "direct_rename",

+      "id": "MR-FS-03",

+      "title": "Directory operations",
+      "tokio_api": "tokio::fs::{create_dir,create_dir_all,remove_dir,read_dir}",

+      "breaking": false
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MR-PR-01",
+      "domain": "process",
+      "title": "Command builder",
+      "tokio_api": "tokio::process::Command",
+      "asupersync_api": "asupersync::process::Command",
+      "change_type": "direct_rename",
+      "breaking": false
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MR-PR-02",
+      "domain": "process",
+      "title": "Child wait methods",
+      "tokio_api": "child.wait().await, child.wait_with_output().await",
+      "asupersync_api": "child.wait() [blocking] or child.wait_async().await",
+      "change_type": "signature_change",
+      "breaking": true,
+      "note": "Blocking by default; use _async variants for async semantics"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MR-PR-03",
+      "domain": "process",
+      "title": "Command output/status",
+      "tokio_api": "command.output().await, command.status().await",
+      "asupersync_api": "command.output() [blocking] or command.output_async().await",
+      "change_type": "signature_change",
+      "breaking": true,
+      "note": "Blocking by default; use _async variants for async semantics"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MR-PR-04",
+      "domain": "process",
+      "title": "Stdio pipe accessors",
+      "tokio_api": "child.stdin.take()",
+      "asupersync_api": "child.stdin()",
+      "change_type": "api_change",
+      "breaking": true,
+      "note": "Method call instead of field access with .take()"

+    {
+      "id": "MR-PR-05",
+      "domain": "process",
+      "title": "Error type",
+      "tokio_api": "std::io::Error",
+      "asupersync_api": "ProcessError {Io, NotFound, PermissionDenied, Signaled}",
+      "change_type": "type_change",
+      "breaking": true,

+    {
+      "id": "MR-SG-01",

@@ -0,0 +1,507 @@
+# Migration Playbook: FS / Process / Signal (Tokio to Asupersync)
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.3.8` ([T3.8])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-04
+**Dependencies**: T3.3 (FS), T3.4 (process), T3.5 (signal), T3.7 (conformance), T3.9 (test matrix), T3.10 (e2e scripts)
+**Purpose**: Self-contained migration guide for users moving from Tokio's fs, process, and signal APIs to their Asupersync equivalents.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Prerequisites
+
+Before migrating, ensure:
+
+1. `asupersync` is added to `Cargo.toml` (minimum version: `0.2.7`)
+2. Remove `tokio` from `[dependencies]` once migration is complete
+3. If using the compatibility crate for incremental migration, add `asupersync-tokio-compat` with relevant features
+
+### 1.1 Feature Flags
+
+| Feature | Purpose |
+|---------|---------|
+| `fs` | Async filesystem operations (default: enabled) |
+| `process` | Child process spawning and lifecycle |
+| `signal` | Unix signal handling and graceful shutdown |
+| `io-uring` | Linux io_uring acceleration for file I/O |
+
+---
+
+## 2. Filesystem Migration
+
+### 2.1 Import Changes
+
+**Before (Tokio)**:
+```rust
+use tokio::fs;
+use tokio::fs::File;
+use tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt, BufReader, BufWriter};
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::fs;
+use asupersync::fs::File;
+use asupersync::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt};
+use asupersync::fs::{BufReader, BufWriter};
+```
+
+### 2.2 File Operations — Direct Equivalents
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync | Notes |
+|-------|------------|-------|
+| `File::open(path)` | `File::open(path)` | Identical signature |
+| `File::create(path)` | `File::create(path)` | Identical signature |
+| `OpenOptions::new()` | `OpenOptions::new()` | Same builder pattern |
+| `file.read(&mut buf)` | `file.read(&mut buf)` | Via `AsyncReadExt` |
+| `file.write_all(data)` | `file.write_all(data)` | Via `AsyncWriteExt` |
+| `file.sync_all()` | `file.sync_all()` | Identical |
+| `file.sync_data()` | `file.sync_data()` | Identical |
+| `file.metadata()` | `file.metadata()` | Identical |
+| `file.seek(pos)` | `file.seek(pos)` | Identical |
+
+### 2.3 Convenience Functions — Direct Equivalents
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync | Notes |
+|-------|------------|-------|
+| `fs::read(path)` | `fs::read(path)` | Both use `spawn_blocking` internally |
+| `fs::read_to_string(path)` | `fs::read_to_string(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::write(path, data)` | `fs::write(path, data)` | Identical |
+| `fs::copy(src, dst)` | `fs::copy(src, dst)` | Identical |
+| `fs::rename(src, dst)` | `fs::rename(src, dst)` | Identical |
+| `fs::remove_file(path)` | `fs::remove_file(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::create_dir(path)` | `fs::create_dir(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::create_dir_all(path)` | `fs::create_dir_all(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::remove_dir(path)` | `fs::remove_dir(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::remove_dir_all(path)` | `fs::remove_dir_all(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::read_dir(path)` | `fs::read_dir(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::metadata(path)` | `fs::metadata(path)` | Identical |
+| `fs::set_permissions(path, p)` | `fs::set_permissions(path, p)` | Identical |
+| `fs::try_exists(path)` | `fs::try_exists(path)` | Identical |
+
+### 2.4 Asupersync-Only Additions
+
+| API | Purpose |
+|-----|---------|
+| `fs::write_atomic(path, data)` | Atomic write via temp file + rename |
+| `fs::BufReader` / `fs::BufWriter` | Async buffered I/O wrappers |
+| `fs::Vfs` trait | Deterministic testing seam (VFS abstraction) |
+| `fs::UnixVfs` | Production VFS implementation |
+| `fs::IoUringFile` | Linux io_uring file I/O (feature: `io-uring`) |
+
+### 2.5 Caveats and Edge Cases
+
+1. **Runtime context required**: `fs::read`, `fs::write`, and other convenience functions use `spawn_blocking_io`, which requires an active Asupersync runtime. Use `File` API (open/create + read/write) for contexts without a runtime (e.g., `futures_lite::future::block_on`).
+
+2. **Symlink behavior**: `fs::symlink_metadata` returns metadata without following symlinks. Behavior matches `std::fs::symlink_metadata`.
+
+3. **Error types**: I/O errors are `std::io::Error`, identical to Tokio.
+
+4. **Cancel safety**: All file operations are cancel-safe. Dropping a future mid-operation discards partial results without corruption.
+
+5. **No `fs::hard_link`**: Use `std::fs::hard_link` via `spawn_blocking` if needed.
+
+---
+
+## 3. Process Migration
+
+### 3.1 Import Changes
+
+**Before (Tokio)**:
+```rust
+use tokio::process::Command;
+use tokio::process::Child;
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::process::{Command, Child, Stdio};
+```
+
+### 3.2 Command Builder — Direct Equivalents
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync | Notes |
+|-------|------------|-------|
+| `Command::new(prog)` | `Command::new(prog)` | Identical |
+| `.arg(a)` | `.arg(a)` | Identical |
+| `.args(list)` | `.args(list)` | Identical |
+| `.env(k, v)` | `.env(k, v)` | Identical |
+| `.envs(pairs)` | `.envs(pairs)` | Identical |
+| `.env_clear()` | `.env_clear()` | Identical |
+| `.env_remove(k)` | `.env_remove(k)` | Identical |
+| `.current_dir(d)` | `.current_dir(d)` | Identical |
+| `.stdin(cfg)` | `.stdin(cfg)` | Identical |
+| `.stdout(cfg)` | `.stdout(cfg)` | Identical |
+| `.stderr(cfg)` | `.stderr(cfg)` | Identical |
+| `.kill_on_drop(b)` | `.kill_on_drop(b)` | Identical |
+| `.spawn()` | `.spawn()` | Identical |
+
+### 3.3 Child Handle — Key Differences
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync | Migration Action |
+|-------|------------|-----------------|
+| `child.id()` returns `Option<u32>` | `child.id()` returns `Option<u32>` | No change |
+| `child.wait().await` (async) | `child.wait()` (blocking) | **Remove `.await`** or use `wait_async().await` |
+| `child.wait_with_output().await` | `child.wait_with_output()` (blocking) | **Remove `.await`** or use `wait_with_output_async().await` |
+| `child.kill().await` | `child.kill()` (sync) | **Remove `.await`** |
+| `child.try_wait()` | `child.try_wait()` | Identical |
+| `Command::output().await` | `Command::output()` (blocking) | **Remove `.await`** or use `output_async().await` |
+| `Command::status().await` | `Command::status()` (blocking) | **Remove `.await`** or use `status_async().await` |
+
+### 3.4 Recommended Migration Pattern for Wait Methods
+
+**Option A — Use blocking methods** (simplest, suitable for non-async contexts):
+```rust
+// Before (Tokio)
+let status = child.wait().await?;
+
+// After (Asupersync) — blocking variant
+let status = child.wait()?;
+```
+
+**Option B — Use async methods** (preserves async semantics):
+```rust
+// After (Asupersync) — async variant
+let status = child.wait_async().await?;
+```
+
+Option B is preferred when the calling context is async and you want to avoid blocking the executor.
+
+### 3.5 Stdio Pipes
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync | Notes |
+|-------|------------|-------|
+| `Stdio::piped()` | `Stdio::piped()` | Identical |
+| `Stdio::inherit()` | `Stdio::inherit()` | Identical |
+| `Stdio::null()` | `Stdio::null()` | Identical |
+| `child.stdin.take()` | `child.stdin()` | **Method call** returns `Option<ChildStdin>` (takes ownership) |
+| `child.stdout.take()` | `child.stdout()` | **Method call** returns `Option<ChildStdout>` (takes ownership) |
+| `child.stderr.take()` | `child.stderr()` | **Method call** returns `Option<ChildStderr>` (takes ownership) |
+
+### 3.6 Error Type Differences
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync |
+|-------|------------|
+| `std::io::Error` | `ProcessError` enum: `Io`, `NotFound`, `PermissionDenied`, `Signaled` |
+
+Migration: Replace `io::Error` handling with `ProcessError` match arms:
+```rust
+match command.spawn() {
+    Ok(child) => { /* ... */ }
+    Err(ProcessError::NotFound(prog)) => eprintln!("Program not found: {prog}"),
+    Err(ProcessError::PermissionDenied(prog)) => eprintln!("Permission denied: {prog}"),
+    Err(ProcessError::Io(e)) => eprintln!("I/O error: {e}"),
+    Err(ProcessError::Signaled(sig)) => eprintln!("Killed by signal: {sig}"),
+}
+```
+
+### 3.7 Caveats
+
+1. **kill_on_drop**: Both Tokio and Asupersync support `kill_on_drop(true)`. In Asupersync, drop sends `SIGKILL` and the process is reaped asynchronously.
+
+2. **No PTY support**: Asupersync does not provide terminal/PTY session APIs. Use the `nix` or `pty-process` crate directly if needed.
+
+3. **Unix-first**: Process APIs are Unix-native. Windows support is not yet at full parity (see gap G12 in the risk register).
+
+4. **Cancellation**: Spawning is synchronous (fork+exec). Cancelling a `wait_async()` future does not kill the child. Combine with `kill_on_drop(true)` for automatic cleanup.
+
+---
+
+## 4. Signal Migration
+
+### 4.1 Import Changes
+
+**Before (Tokio)**:
+```rust
+use tokio::signal;
+use tokio::signal::unix::{signal as unix_signal, SignalKind};
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::signal::{self, Signal, SignalKind};
+use asupersync::signal::{ShutdownController, ShutdownReceiver};
+use asupersync::signal::{with_graceful_shutdown, GracefulOutcome};
+```
+
+### 4.2 Signal Listening
+
+**Before (Tokio)**:
+```rust
+let mut sigterm = tokio::signal::unix::signal(SignalKind::terminate())?;
+sigterm.recv().await;
+println!("Got SIGTERM");
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+let mut sigterm = asupersync::signal::signal(SignalKind::Terminate)?;
+sigterm.recv().await;
+println!("Got SIGTERM");
+```
+
+Key difference: `SignalKind` uses enum variants (`SignalKind::Terminate`) instead of constructor functions (`SignalKind::terminate()`).
+
+### 4.3 Ctrl+C Handling
+
+**Before (Tokio)**:
+```rust
+tokio::signal::ctrl_c().await?;
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+asupersync::signal::ctrl_c()?;
+// Or use the signal stream:
+let mut sigint = asupersync::signal::sigint()?;
+sigint.recv().await;
+```
+
+### 4.4 Graceful Shutdown — Asupersync Pattern
+
+Asupersync provides a structured shutdown primitive that has no direct Tokio equivalent:
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::signal::{ShutdownController, with_graceful_shutdown, GracefulOutcome};
+
+let controller = ShutdownController::new();
+let receiver = controller.subscribe();
+
+// Run a task with graceful shutdown awareness
+let outcome = with_graceful_shutdown(
+    std::pin::pin!(async {
+        // Your main work here
+        do_work().await
+    }),
+    receiver,
+).await;
+
+match outcome {
+    GracefulOutcome::Completed(result) => println!("Task finished: {result:?}"),
+    GracefulOutcome::ShutdownSignaled => println!("Shutdown requested, task interrupted"),
+}
+```
+
+### 4.5 SignalKind Mapping
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync |
+|-------|------------|
+| `SignalKind::terminate()` | `SignalKind::Terminate` |
+| `SignalKind::interrupt()` | `SignalKind::Interrupt` |
+| `SignalKind::hangup()` | `SignalKind::Hangup` |
+| `SignalKind::quit()` | `SignalKind::Quit` |
+| `SignalKind::user_defined1()` | `SignalKind::User1` |
+| `SignalKind::user_defined2()` | `SignalKind::User2` |
+| `SignalKind::child()` | `SignalKind::Child` |
+| `SignalKind::window_change()` | `SignalKind::WindowChange` |
+| `SignalKind::pipe()` | `SignalKind::Pipe` |
+| `SignalKind::alarm()` | `SignalKind::Alarm` |
+
+### 4.6 Convenience Functions
+
+| Tokio | Asupersync |
+|-------|------------|
+| `signal::unix::signal(kind)` | `signal::signal(kind)` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigint()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigterm()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sighup()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigusr1()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigusr2()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigquit()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigchld()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigpipe()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigalrm()` |
+| (none) | `signal::sigwinch()` |
+
+### 4.7 Caveats
+
+1. **Unix-only**: Signal APIs return `Err` on non-Unix platforms. Gate with `#[cfg(unix)]`.
+
+2. **Cancel safety**: `Signal::recv()` and `ShutdownReceiver::wait()` are cancel-safe.
+
+3. **Signal coalescing**: Multiple signals delivered before `recv()` is polled are coalesced into a single notification (same as Tokio).
+
+4. **No `from_raw`**: Asupersync does not support `SignalKind::from_raw(i32)`. Use the named variants.
+
+---
+
+## 5. Cross-Domain Patterns
+
+### 5.1 Process + Signal: Graceful Child Shutdown
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::process::{Command, Stdio};
+use asupersync::signal::{ShutdownController, with_graceful_shutdown, GracefulOutcome};
+
+async fn run_server_with_shutdown() {
+    let controller = ShutdownController::new();
+    let receiver = controller.subscribe();
+
+    let mut child = Command::new("my-server")
+        .kill_on_drop(true)
+        .spawn()
+        .expect("spawn");
+
+    let outcome = with_graceful_shutdown(
+        std::pin::pin!(async {
+            child.wait_async().await
+        }),
+        receiver,
+    ).await;
+
+    match outcome {
+        GracefulOutcome::Completed(status) => {
+            println!("Server exited: {status:?}");
+        }
+        GracefulOutcome::ShutdownSignaled => {
+            let _ = child.kill();
+            println!("Server terminated by shutdown signal");
+        }
+    }
+}
+```
+
+### 5.2 FS + Signal: Flush-Before-Exit
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::fs;
+use asupersync::io::AsyncWriteExt;
+use asupersync::signal::{ShutdownController, with_graceful_shutdown, GracefulOutcome};
+
+async fn write_with_shutdown(path: &std::path::Path) {
+    let controller = ShutdownController::new();
+    let receiver = controller.subscribe();
+
+    let outcome = with_graceful_shutdown(
+        std::pin::pin!(async {
+            let mut file = fs::File::create(path).await.expect("create");
+            file.write_all(b"important data").await.expect("write");
+            file.sync_all().await.expect("sync");
+            true
+        }),
+        receiver,
+    ).await;
+
+    match outcome {
+        GracefulOutcome::Completed(true) => println!("Data safely persisted"),
+        GracefulOutcome::ShutdownSignaled => println!("Shutdown before write completed"),
+        _ => unreachable!(),
+    }
+}
+```
+
+### 5.3 FS + Process: Capture Output to File
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::process::{Command, Stdio};
+use asupersync::fs;
+
+async fn capture_to_file(cmd: &str, output_path: &std::path::Path) {
+    let output = Command::new(cmd)
+        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
+        .output()
+        .expect("run command");
+
+    // Write captured stdout to file atomically
+    fs::write_atomic(output_path, &output.stdout)
+        .await
+        .expect("write output");
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## 6. Troubleshooting
+
+### 6.1 Common Migration Errors
+
+| Error | Cause | Fix |
+|-------|-------|-----|
+| `method wait is not async` | Asupersync `wait()` is blocking | Use `wait_async().await` or remove `.await` |
+| `no method named take` on Child | Stdio accessors are methods, not fields | Change `child.stdin.take()` to `child.stdin()` |
+| `expected ProcessError, found io::Error` | Error type changed | Match on `ProcessError` variants |
+| `SignalKind::terminate() not found` | Enum variant, not function | Use `SignalKind::Terminate` |
+| `fs::read failed with NotFound` | No runtime context for `spawn_blocking_io` | Use `File` API directly, or ensure runtime is active |
+| `ctrl_c is not async` | Signature difference | Call `ctrl_c()?` without `.await` |
+
+### 6.2 Rollback Strategy
+
+If migration encounters blocking issues:
+
+1. **Incremental**: Use `asupersync-tokio-compat` to bridge specific callsites while keeping Tokio for the rest
+2. **Per-module**: Migrate one domain at a time (signal first, then process, then FS)
+3. **Feature-flag**: Gate Asupersync usage behind a Cargo feature for A/B comparison
+
+### 6.3 Verification Checklist
+
+After migration, verify:
+
+- [ ] All `#[tokio::test]` attributes replaced with appropriate test runner
+- [ ] No remaining `tokio::fs`, `tokio::process`, or `tokio::signal` imports
+- [ ] `kill_on_drop` behavior tested with actual process spawning
+- [ ] Graceful shutdown tested end-to-end
+- [ ] Error handling updated for `ProcessError` enum
+- [ ] Cancel safety verified for critical paths
+
+---
+
+## 7. Platform Notes
+
+### 7.1 Linux
+
+Full support for all FS, process, and signal APIs. Optional io_uring acceleration available via `io-uring` feature flag.
+
+### 7.2 macOS
+
+Full support for FS and process. Signal support is complete for all listed `SignalKind` variants. No io_uring support (Linux-only).
+
+### 7.3 Windows
+
+- **FS**: Full support
+- **Process**: Core spawn/wait/kill supported. Some Unix-specific features (signal delivery, process groups) unavailable
+- **Signal**: Limited to `ctrl_c()`. Other signal types return `Err` with `ErrorKind::Unsupported`
+
